prompt:
Billing found adjustments from one customer's invoice batch showing up on a different customer's export. I honestly suspected the export service was caching payloads on their side, but I can reproduce it entirely offline with just our invoicebatch module, so it's ours. Make two batches, add a late fee to the first, and the second batch suddenly has it too — total_cents and the serialized JSON both show the foreign line. It also looks like editing a payload dict after to_payload() reaches back into the batch, and the retry helper gets weirder the longer the process lives: by the third submission in one process it starts erroring out instead of retrying.

Contract, to be explicit: batches are independent unless you deliberately share state; a list passed to the constructor stays the caller's (we don't mutate theirs, they can't mutate ours); to_payload()/serialize() are point-in-time snapshots; submit_with_retry gets max_attempts fresh tries on every call and logs (batch_id, attempt) pairs numbered from 1 into whatever log list the caller passes — or into its own if they don't pass one. The constructor and function signatures are public API; keep them compatible.

`python3 test_invoicebatch.py` pins all of the above and fails today. Tests are off-limits.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0 on the buggy fixture; test_invoicebatch.py exits 1 with AssertionError in the isolation check: a batch constructed with no adjustments shows another batch's late-fee lines; the per-call retry-budget scenario additionally dies with UnboundLocalError once the shared default log fills
